channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: Lefthand Warmoth `51 Precision Bass Mitte der 90er Schaller Seymour Duncan Antiquity CTS — $1,056.35
item.description:
$1,056.35 · Good · Michael's Boutique

Zum Verkauf steht ein hochwertiger Linkshänder-Bass aus Warmoth-Komponenten, der dem klassischen "Ur-Preci" von 1951 nachempfunden ist. Das Instrument kombiniert Vintage-Vibe mit moderner Bespielbarkeit. Das Gewicht sind 4,55 kg.

  • Korpus & Hals: Warmoth (Lefthand), Farbe: Transparent Blonde / Vintage White.
  • Hals-Features: Ahornhals mit einem zusätzlichen 21. Bund (Griffbretterweiterung).
  • Pickups: Seymour Duncan Antiquity (Single Coil für den authentischen 50s-Knurren).
  • Elektronik: Hochwertige CTS Potis und Orange Drop Kondensator für erstklassige Tonkontrolle.
  • Hardware: Schaller Mechaniken für optimale Stimmstabilität.
  • Besonderheit: Der obere Gurtpin wurde an der Zarge (nahe Halstasche) montiert, die ursprünglichen Löcher an den Hörnern sind vorhanden. Auf der Kopfplatte befindet sich ein Fender-Decal (vom Vorbesitzer angebracht). 

Ein charakterstarker Bass für Lefty-Player, die den Sound der frühen 50er suchen!

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Up for sale is a high-quality left-handed bass built from Warmoth parts, inspired by the legendary 1951 "Ur-Precision" design. This instrument blends vintage aesthetics with modern performance. The weight is 4,55 kg.

  • Body & Neck: Warmoth (Left-hand), Finish: Transparent Blonde / Vintage White.
  • Neck Features: Maple neck featuring an additional 21st fret (fretboard extension).
  • Pickups: Seymour Duncan Antiquity (Single coil for that authentic 50s growl).
  • Electronics: Premium CTS pots and Orange Drop capacitor for superior tone shaping.
  • Hardware: Schaller tuners for rock-solid tuning stability.
  • Note: The upper strap button is mounted on the side near the neck pocket; original holes on the horns are present. The headstock features a Fender decal (applied by a previous owner). 

A soulful bass for southpaw players looking for that iconic early 50s tone!

item.title: Gibson EB-3L Slotted Headstock 1971 Natural Vintage Project — $2,318.31
item.description:
$2,318.31 · Fair · Michael's Boutique

1971 Gibson EB-3L (Long Scale) – Slotted Headstock – Natural – Vintage Projekt

Beschreibung:
Ich verkaufe einen seltenen Gibson EB-3L aus dem Jahr 1971 (Seriennummer 67909X, Poti-Codes von 1971). Es handelt sich um das gesuchte Modell mit „Slotted Headstock“ (Fensterkopfplatte) in der Long-Scale-Ausführung. Der Bass wiegt sensationell leichte 3,4 kg.

Zustand & Wartungsbedarf:
Der Bass ist ein echter „Survivor“ im Natural-Finish mit deutlicher Patina. Zwischen den Pickups gibt es eine Spielspur bis auf das Holz (ca. 1,5 x 3 cm).
Wichtige Hinweise für Käufer: Der Bass benötigt ein technisches Setup. Die Bolzen der Brücke sind leicht nach vorne geneigt (Gibson-typisch), der Hals weist eine Vorwärtswölbung auf und sollte justiert werden. Der 4-fach Drehschalter arbeitet in Pos. 2 & 3 gut, Pos. 1 & 4 müssen bewusst eingerastet werden.

Zubehör:
Inklusive originalem Gibson-Formkoffer. Dieser ist stark mitgenommen (mit Panzerband stabilisiert, Griff durch Stoffwicklung ersetzt), erfüllt aber seinen Zweck für den Transport. Die originale Pickup-Kappe mit Dämpfer und Schrauben ist dabei. Ein ehrliches Vintage-Instrument für Kenner, die ihm mit etwas Liebe wieder zu altem Glanz verhelfen wollen.

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________

1971 Gibson EB-3L (Long Scale) – Slotted Headstock – Natural – Player's Project

Description:
For sale is a 1971 Gibson EB-3L with the iconic slotted headstock. Confirmed by serial number 67909X and 1971 pot codes. This long-scale mahogany bass is exceptionally lightweight at only 3.4 kg (7.5 lbs).

Condition & Maintenance Notes:
The bass features a beautiful, worn Natural finish with authentic playwear (notably a worn spot between the pickups).
Please note: This instrument is sold as a project and needs a professional setup. The bridge posts are slightly leaning forward (common Gibson issue), and the neck has a forward bow that requires adjustment. The 4-way rotary switch works perfectly in positions 2 & 3, while 1 & 4 need careful engagement.

Hardware & Case:
Includes the original bridge cover/mute with screws. The original Gibson hardshell case is included but is in "well-loved" condition (repaired with duct tape, makeshift cloth handle). A great opportunity to own a rare Slothead EB-3L with tons of character, ready for a refresh!

item.title: ESP LTD AP-204 2019 - Present - Black Satin — $571.95
item.description:
$571.95 · Brand New · Musical Pontevedra

El ESP LTD AP-204 es un bajo eléctrico de 4 cuerdas que combina el estilo clásico de los bajos tipo Precision/Jazz con las prestaciones modernas características de ESP. Diseñado para ofrecer comodidad, versatilidad y un excelente rendimiento, es una opción ideal para bajistas principiantes avanzados que buscan un instrumento confiable.


Su cuerpo de caoba proporciona un sonido cálido y con gran sustain, mientras que el mástil de arce con perfil Thin U ofrece una ejecución rápida y cómoda. La configuración de pastillas ESP Designed LDP (Split Coil) + LDJ (Single Coil) junto con un ecualizador activo de 2 bandas (ABQ-2S) permite obtener desde graves profundos y contundentes hasta sonidos brillantes y definidos, adaptándose a prácticamente cualquier estilo musical.

Especificaciones:

  • Modelo: ESP LTD AP-204
  • Tipo: Bajo eléctrico de 4 cuerdas
  • Construcción: Bolt-On (mástil atornillado)
  • Escala: 34" (864 mm)
  • Cuerpo: Caoba (Mahogany)
  • Mástil: Arce (Maple)
  • Perfil del mástil: Thin U
  • Diapasón: Jatoba tostado (Roasted Jatoba)
  • Radio del diapasón: 400 mm (15.75")
  • Número de trastes: 21 Extra Jumbo
  • Ancho de cejuela: 38 mm Cejuela Moldeada (Molded)
  • Pastilla central: ESP Designed LDP Split Coil
  • Pastilla puente: ESP Designed LDJ Single Coil
  • Configuración: PJ (Precision + Jazz)
  • Electrónica: Activa Ecualizador ESP ABQ-2S de 2 bandas (Graves/Agudos)
  • Controles: Volumen, Balance, Graves y Agudos
  • Puente: LTD DB-4
  • Clavijeros: LTD Die-Cast
  • Hardware: Cromado
  • Cuerdas de fábrica: D'Addario XL165 (.045-.105)

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass Special - PJ-535 80s - Black — $1,428.48
item.description:
$1,428.48 · Excellent · Hot Rod Guitars Lab SHOP

Up for sale is a very rare Fender Jazz Bass Special model PJ-535, from the legendary "Boxer" series produced in Japan at the historic Fujigen factory during the mid-1980s (E-series serial number).

This instrument is in excellent aesthetic and functional condition and is 100% original in every part. No modifications, no repairs, and no refrets.

Key Features:

  • Medium Scale (32"): A true rarity for Fender. It is incredibly comfortable, lightweight, and fast to play. Perfect for players with medium-to-small hands or anyone looking for a modern, agile feel.
  • 100% Original Electronics & Pickups: Features the iconic PJ configuration with the characteristic slanted pickups unique to the Boxer series. It delivers a punchy attack and an unmistakable rock growl.
  • Original Controls: All original pots, knobs, and wiring are untouched and work flawlessly with no scratchy noise.
  • Excellent Condition: The finish (including the iconic glossy all-black neck back) looks stunning for a nearly 40-year-old instrument. The frets have plenty of life left.
  • Original Hardware: Complete with all its original black hardware and factory-correct black knobs.

Details & Dating:

The neck heel features the original factory-correct "PJ" stamps from the Fujigen production line. The headstock serial number (E808651) dates this bass to the golden era of "Made in Japan" production (1985–1987).

This is a killer-looking, "total black" piece of Japanese luthiery history—highly collectible and very hard to find in this clean, unmodified state.

item.title: 【new】HEX Guitars / B100R G/SB【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $703
item.description:
$703 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

HEX Guitars is a South Korea-based brand specializing in entry-level to mid-range guitars.

They own their own factory in Indonesia and manufacture their guitars themselves. This sets them apart from brands that typically use OEM manufacturing.

Their passion is so strong that the boss himself is involved in the production process, resulting in guitars of a quality far exceeding what one might expect from their price point.

The B series features models that blend classic character with modern specifications.

This B100 is a modern 4-string JB model.

The body is made of Indonesian Mahogany.

Indonesian Mahogany is highly valued in recent years for its weight and tone, which are well-suited to musical instruments.

The stable supply of such high-quality wood in Indonesia makes it one of the most logical locations for high-quality instrument manufacturing.

The neck is made of Maple.

Two carbon fiber rods are incorporated to enhance its strength.

The fretboard is made of Indian rosewood, and the fretboard radius is a relatively flat 12 inches.

The neck has a thin, easy-to-grip C-shape.

The scale length is the most common 34” scale.

The pickups are original HEX® Alnico Single Coil pickups.

They use original pickups in many of their models.

This isn't for cost-cutting, but because they have confidence in the quality of their self-designed pickups.

The staff, who understand music and play it regularly, thoroughly research many elements such as magnets, wires, and bobbins before designing them.

What you'll notice when you hold it is its high playability.
It's extremely easy to play and stress-free.

The sound is truly classic JB.
It has a full, resonant, warm midrange, and provides a solid bottom end in an ensemble.

The high quality of the basic neck components contributes to its excellent playability, and it's difficult to say that it's inferior to more expensive instruments.

Overall, it's a superb product with high quality in looks, playability, and sound.

●SPECS

BODY
MATERIAL: Indonesian Mahogany
SHAPE: HEX® B
FINISH: Gloss

NECK
MATERIAL: Maple
SHAPE : "C"
SCALE: 864mm(34")
TRUSS ROD : Spoke Nut Type
ASSEMBLY : 4 Bolt Steel Square Plate

FRET BOARD
MATERIAL : Indian Rosewood
FINISH: Light Matte
FRETS: 21, Nickel
RADIUS: 12"
NUT: Bone
INLAY : Pearloid Dot, 6mm

HEAD
SHAPE : HEX® Original
TUNERS : Vintage Open Gear

PICKUPS
CONFIGURATION : Single + Single
CONTROLS: 2-Volume, 1-Tone
NECK PICKUP : HEX® Alnico Single Coil
BRIDGE PICKUP : HEX® Alnico Single Coil

HARDWARE
BRIDGE : Vintage Type 4-Saddle
STRING: Nickel Plated Steel (.045-.105 Gauges)
CASE: Deluxe Gigbag

item.title: 2023 Marleaux MBass 5-String Movingui Friso Signature Top - Natural | German Custom Single Cut Bass Delano | CoA Case — $4,521.15
item.description:
$4,521.15 · Mint · Simon's Gear Garage

I am offering a Marleaux MBass 5 from 2023, a custom 5-string luxury bass model handmade in the legendary noble bass forge of Gerald Marleaux in Clausthal-Zellerfeld (Germany) with an extremely resonant mahogany body, selected Movingui-Friso Signature top (!), two-piece mahogany/rosewood neck double-reinforced with maple, fine santos fingerboard with side dot inlays and 24 frets, two hot-warm Delano SBE driver pickups, black ETS Custom bridge and knobs as well as black Schaller tuners in a natural matte finish in mint condition including Marleaux Deluxe Soft Case, all manufacturer's papers (incl. C.o.A.) and professional setup with Thomastik flatwound strings.



Under the label "Marleaux Bass Guitars", Gerald Marleaux has been hand-building custom basses of the finest class in Clausthal-Zellerfeld since 1990, which are now among the most sought-after European instruments of all. Marleaux basses combine the best in hardware and extravagant woods with a style of instrument making that pays great attention to detail, creating instruments that are in a league of their own in terms of appearance, sound and playability.


And this Marleaux MBass 5 is no exception: it comes with an exquisitely figured mahogany body in an old-shape design (i.e. including the "Mickey Mouse ear") with a special mahogany neck including a double maple-reinfoced rosewood center strip in neck thru construction. In addition to super sustain, this allowes access to 2 full octaves on the elegant Santos fingerboard. As a special extra, this MBass 5 also has a signature top made of spectacularly figured Movingui Friso, which complements the black ETS custom hardware and the dark Schaller tuners perfectly.


Thanks to the choice of tonewoods, the instrument resonates audibly and incredibly from the very first note and, at the latest when you plug in the bass, you need a moment to collect yourself from the sheer load of growling, powerful bass tones. This is ensured by two Delano SBE Driver humbucker pickups in a class of their own, which give the bass a warm-balanced sound character with great clarity and an intense, direct response. Thanks to the pickup coil switch, the pickups can be switched in series, parallel or as a single coil, allowing for a wide variety of sounds.


A sound example of the model (with other tonewoods) can be found at the end of the photo gallery above.


If you are looking for a handmade custom luxury bass that combines perfection in sound, look and playability, you will find it here!


Technical specifications:

  • Origin: Clausthal-Zellerfeld, Lower Saxony, Germany
  • Year of manufacture: 2023
  • Design: 5-String Singlecut Neck Thru
  • Body: Mahogany, solid
  • Top: Selected Movingui-Friso Signature Top
  • Neck: Full-length mahogany neck with rosewood center stripe, double-reinforced with maple; additional carbon-reinforcement
  • Fingerboard: Santos rosewood
  • Fretboard radius: 12''-16'' compound
  • Position markers: White side dots
  • Pickups: 2x Delano SBE Driver Humbucker
  • Electronics: 1x Master Vol., 2x Tone, 2x pickup coil switch (serial, parallel, single-coil)
  • Bridge: ETS Black
  • Tuners: Schaller Black
  • Finish: Natural, matt
  • Frets: 24 Medium
  • Scale length: Long Scale (864mm)
  • Nut width: 46mm
  • Specials: Old Shape (incl. "Mickey Mouse Ear"), Siganture Top
  • Weight: 4.0kg
  • Serial no.: 2623
  • Incl. Marleaux soft case, manufacturer's papers (incl. C.o.A.) and accessories


This Marleaux Diva 5 is in mint condition and technically flawless. Visually there is also no wear (see pictures).


The bass comes with professional setup with Thomastik JF 345 flatwound strings and I (a longtime guitar and bass teacher) will ship fully insured with DHL (incl. bulky goods surcharge) for 69.99€ within Germany or for 119,99€ within the EU. Shipping outside the EU possible - individual shipping costs on request.


All images shown are of the actual instrument for sale.

item.description:
$749 · Excellent · Vegas Guitars

We are a Fender Gold authorized service center and made this bass from brand new Fender parts. It has a roasted maple neck and alder body in Olympic white. 3 ply vintage tortoise shell pickguard, metal precision knobs - All Fender parts and pickups. Tuners, pickups and pickguards still have protective plastic - never owned or used. Plays amazingly well. Neck serial is MX22287718

This is an excellent buy - a new instrument setup by pros. If you're on a budget this is the way to go!

item.title: ATELIER Z USED ​​M#245 Custom with MCT375 (NAT/Black Line) — $1,683.04
item.description:
$1,683.04 · Good · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

While based on the 70s Fender JB, this bass incorporates numerous specifications and design elements that reflect a bassist's perspective, such as an original body shape, the installation of the unique "Z Tuning System" with a comfortable, moderate buzz, the powerful active sound from the Bartolini XTCT, and a thick clear pickguard and pickup fence that smoothly control the thumbing and pulling techniques that are its signature slap style.

This instrument boasts a dynamic and powerful bass thanks to its ash body, which features a wood grain pattern brought to life by the application of ink to the wood pores. It also offers a pleasingly tight tone and a fast, responsive attack thanks to its maple neck. You can enjoy the dynamic, clear sound characteristic of 70's JB basses, delivered with a supple touch—a true player's gear. Furthermore, the rich vibration produced by the full-size body and the open resonance resulting from the traditional neck construction (without graphite support) contribute to a pleasant, rustic feel, which is another key feature of this model. Additionally, this model is equipped with an MCT375 middle control, allowing for a wide range of sound shaping with its 3-band EQ.

Although there is some tarnishing, minor scratches, and light dents on the parts, the guitar is in relatively good condition. However, the pickguard and control panel appear to be fixed with adhesive and cannot be removed, so the adhesive surface is visible.
The 4th string tuning peg was damaged, so we replaced it with a new one at our shop.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, the neck condition is good with no major issues and minimal fret wear.

Weight: ≈4.02kg
Accessories: Original gig bag, spec sheet

item.title: Hofner 500/1 Custom Vintage 64 Left - Höfner limitiert — $2,379.62
item.description:
$2,379.62 · Very Good · Niklas's Gear Outlet

Description (English/German below)


Title:
Höfner Violin Bass Left Hand – Custom Shop Special Order (1964 + Cavern Specs) incl. Certificate

Description:

For sale is an official Höfner custom-built instrument in left-hand configuration – not a modification, but a factory-built, individually specified bass.

This instrument combines 1964 specifications with Cavern features, bringing together two of the most sought-after Höfner characteristics:

  • Cavern pickup position for increased attack and definition
  • 1964 construction / tonal character for a warm, woody vintage sound
  • Deluxe-style pickguard for a more refined and unique appearance

The result is an exceptionally versatile violin bass with a classic Beatles vibe – a true one-of-a-kind instrument both sonically and visually.

Specifications:

  • Left-handed electric bass
  • Top: Spruce
  • Back & sides: Flamed maple
  • Body binding (top/back): Black/white/black
  • Neck: 3-piece maple
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood (Dalbergia Latifolia)
  • Neck binding: White
  • Scale length: 762 mm (30")
  • Nut width: 42 mm
  • 22 frets
  • Bridge: Ebony
  • Tuners: Strip nickel
  • Tailpiece: Höfner trapeze nickel
  • Pickups: 2x Höfner staple (nickel)
  • Electronics: Höfner black panel with gold top knobs
  • Pickguard: Black / Deluxe-style
  • Finish: Sunburst
  • Made in Germany
  • Includes original case

Highlights:

  • Official Höfner custom-built instrument (left hand)
  • Combination of 1964 and Cavern specifications
  • Deluxe-style pickguard
  • Original invoice & certificate included
  • No aftermarket modifications – factory original build
  • Very well maintained condition

Sound & Playability:

Classic Höfner tone with a unique balance of warmth and clarity:

  • round, woody, and vintage in character
  • more direct and articulate compared to standard ’64-style models
  • ideal for Beatles, 60s, indie, and soul

Shipping / Pickup:

  • Secure worldwide shipping available
  • Local pickup also possible


——————- german ————————-

Title:
Höfner Violin Bass Left Hand – Custom Shop Sonderanfertigung (1964 + Cavern Specs) inkl. Zertifikat

Zum Verkauf steht eine offizielle Sonderanfertigung von Höfner in Left Hand Ausführung – kein Umbau, sondern ein individuell konfiguriertes Instrument.

Der Bass kombiniert gezielt 1964er Spezifikationen mit Cavern-Features und vereint damit zwei der begehrtesten Höfner-Charakteristika:

  • Cavern Pickup-Position für mehr Direktheit und Attack
  • 1964er Bauweise / Toncharakter für den warmen, holzigen Vintage-Sound
  • Deluxe-Style Pickguard für eine edlere, seltene Optik

Das Ergebnis ist ein außergewöhnlich vielseitiger Violin Bass mit klassischem Beatles-Vibe – klanglich wie optisch ein echtes Einzelstück.

Specifications:

  • Linkshänder E-Bass
  • Decke: Fichte
  • Boden & Zargen: geflammter Ahorn
  • Binding Decke/Boden: schwarz/weiß/schwarz
  • Hals: 3-teilig Ahorn
  • Griffbrett: Palisander (Dalbergia Latifolia)
  • Binding Hals: weiß
  • Mensur: 762 mm (30")
  • Sattelbreite: 42 mm
  • 22 Bünde
  • Brücke: Ebenholz
  • Mechaniken: Strip Nickel
  • Tailpiece: Höfner Trapeze Nickel
  • Tonabnehmer: 2x Höfner Staple (Nickel)
  • Elektronik: Höfner Black Panel mit Gold Top Knobs
  • Pickguard: Black / Deluxe-Style
  • Finish: Sunburst
  • Made in Germany
  • inkl. original Koffer

Besonderheiten:

  • Offizielle Höfner Sonderanfertigung (Left Hand)
  • Kombination aus 1964- und Cavern-Spezifikationen
  • Deluxe-Style Pickguard
  • Originalrechnung & Zertifikat vorhanden (werden mitgeliefert)
  • Kein nachträglicher Umbau – original so gebaut
  • Sehr gepflegter Zustand

Sound & Spielgefühl:

Typischer Höfner-Sound mit einer besonderen Mischung aus Wärme und Definition:

  • rund, holzig und vintage im Grundcharakter
  • gleichzeitig direkter und durchsetzungsfähiger durch die Cavern-Spezifikation
  • ideal für Beatles, 60s, Indie, Soul

Versand / Abholung:

  • Sicherer, gut verpackter Versand weltweit möglich
  • Abholung ebenfalls möglich

item.title: Warrior Dran Michael DM250 6 String — $5,374.83
item.description:
$5,374.83 · Excellent · Sydney City Vintage

This is a handcrafted boutique instrument that embodies Warrior's reputation for exceptional craftsmanship, premium tonewoods, and uncompromising attention to detail. Built in the USA, the DMB250 is designed for players who demand outstanding tonal versatility, effortless playability, and striking aesthetics.

I have reached out to Warrior for more information on this exact build, however I have not heard back from them.

This does, however, feature a beautifully sculpted body, a fast-playing neck, and an extended 6-string range, and delivers incredible clarity across the entire fretboard. Whether you're laying down deep, articulate low-end or exploring expressive chordal and solo passages, this bass responds with remarkable balance, sustain, and dynamic sensitivity.

Like many Warrior custom instruments, this example showcases carefully selected exotic woods and high-end electronics, producing a rich, organic voice with the flexibility to cover everything from gospel and jazz to fusion, progressive rock, and modern contemporary styles. Its boutique construction ensures excellent resonance and a comfortable playing experience, making it equally at home on stage, in the studio, or as part of a discerning collection.

Every Warrior is unique, and looking closely at this example, the wood is so incredibly beautiful, you definitely notice something new every time you take a look.

This was purchased from a shop in QLD (Australia), and never used, barley taken out of the case. I am presenting this as a "new/never used" instrument, and it still has all of the price tags from the store, as well as the "DO NOT TOUCH" sign (haha).

Certainly a very exciting artwork of a bass, ready to find a new home in the pocket.

item.title: Freedom Custom Guitar Research Dulake Flat 4-string Ruri (RRI) [19042023P] (07/02) — $3,466.21
item.description:
$3,466.21 · Very Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Wefve just received a beautiful pre-owned Freedom Custom Guitar Research Dulake Flat-4st!

The body features a laminated construction of Premium Figured Maple, Wenge, and 2-piece Alder. The clear attack and prominent highs of the figured maple, the tight mid-to-low frequencies of the wenge, and the natural, easy-to-play tone of the alder blend together to create a superbly balanced sound. This design offers the wide dynamic range expected of a high-end bass while still delivering a distinct musical warmth.

The neck is crafted from maple and incorporates Freedomfs proprietary gARIMIZO & One Point Jointh construction. This creates a strong sense of unity between the neck and body, contributing to fast attack and rich sustain. Additionally, the neck features the FCGR FAT U profile. It offers a firm grip while naturally conforming to the hand during play, providing a stable feel even during extended playing sessions.

The guitar is equipped with FCGR Stainless SP-SF-09 Warm frets. These frets offer the exceptional durability characteristic of stainless steel while, as the name suggests, providing a warm texture; they are characterized by a natural sound that avoids excessive harshness. Furthermore, the use of a Real Carbon Nut ensures uniform resonance from open strings to high positions, along with stable tuning performance.

The guitar is equipped with the FCGR Original Bass HB PU 4st pickup, which, when combined with the original preamp, delivers a wide range of sounds?from a thick, powerful humbucker tone to subtle nuances. It balances clear definition with rich low-end response, making it a versatile instrument ready for use across various genres.

Weighing in at 3.73 kg, it is exceptionally lightweight for a multi-layered body in this class. It offers excellent playability and reduces strain during performance, all without compromising on sound quality in the slightest.

The eye-catching Ruri (RRI) finish highlights the premium-grade figured maple, dyed a vivid blue, allowing you to fully appreciate the three-dimensional depth and richness of the wood grain.

After purchase, the guitar was cleaned and set up at our repair center, so it is ready to play right away.

BODY : Premium Figured Maple/Wenge/Alder 2P
NECK : Maple
JOINT : ARIMIZO & One-Point Joint
GRIP : FCGR FAT U
FINGERBOARD : Indian Rosewood, 24 frets, 34-inch scale (400R)
NUT : Real Carbon Nut
FRETS : FCGR Stainless SP-SF-09 Warm
FINISH : Ruri (RRI)
PICKUP : FCGR Original Bass HB PU 4-string
WEIGHT : 3.73 kg
DATE : Jan. 2019

Condition: A: Excellent Condition

Serial Number: 19042023P
Year of Manufacture: -2019
Country of Manufacture: Japan
Case: Semi-hard case
Accessories: Wrench, Spec Sheet
Weight: 3.77 kg

item.description:
$2,176.01 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A 5-string bass from the CS series, an original model by Dragonfly?a Japanese brand known for its distinctive designs and popular among professional musicians.
Featuring a beautifully figured maple top and an alder back, the body is compact and easy to hold, with a rounded shape that offers an exceptional fit.
The neck is made of maple with a satin finish, and the fingerboard is likely rosewood or pearwood.
Equipped with a bridge compatible with both through-body and top-loading string routing, it allows for individual string tension adjustment, and the deep cutaway ensures excellent playability on the higher frets.
A comprehensive set of controls?including master volume, balancer, tone, 3-band EQ, mid-frequency control, and an active/passive switch?enables a wide range of sound shaping.

There are several small dents accompanied by paint chips, cracks, and dents at the headstock angle, as well as paint chips on the lower back of the body and the horn sections.

item.title: ATELIER Z M#265/18p Natural [502406] (07/02) — $2,253.04
item.description:
$2,253.04 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

ATELIER Z Guitar Works M#265/18p Natural

We have received a used "M#265/18p" from the MAGNITUDE series by "Atelier Z," a Japanese brand favored by various artists both in Japan and abroad.

The M#265 is the 5-string version of the M#245, a model favored by players who frequently use slap technique.
It features the classic sound character of a jazz bass, capable of handling all musical styles, while inheriting the clear projection characteristic of vintage models. It also successfully brings out the distinct attack of slap playing with clarity. This model is crafted from materials that can keep pace with the wide dynamic range of high-end amplifiers.

This JB-type model, equipped with an active circuit, features a two-piece ash body and a one-piece maple neck, connected via a four-point detachable joint.
The nut width is 47.5 mm, with a 34-inch long scale, a 400R fingerboard radius, block position markers, and 21 medium-jumbo frets.
The g18ph in the model name refers to a string-to-string width of 18 mm.
The hardware is primarily chrome; it is equipped with Gotoh GB528-5 tuners and an ATELIER Z BB518 bridge.

It is equipped with two gATELIER Z JBZ-5/18ph pickups and a gBartolini XTCTh preamp. Controls include Master Volume (Balance), Master Tone, Active/Passive Switch, Middle Boost, Treble Control, and Bass Control.

The guitar is in good overall condition; the truss rod rotates 60 degrees in both directions, and approximately 80% of the frets remain.

The finish is gNatural,h which highlights the ash grain, with a glossy finish and an acrylic clear pickguard.

S/N: 502406
Weight: 4.25 kg
Accessories: Non-original soft case

-Neck-
Scale: 34" (864 mm)
Neck Material

-Fingerboard-
Material Solid Maple
Fingerboard Radius 400R
Fret Dimensions W2.7mm/H1.0mm
Frets 21 frets
Nut Material Bone
Nut Width 47.5mm
Finish Gloss. Neck Back = Matte

-Body-
Body Material Ash 2P
Body Shape JB "M" Type (Original "J" Body)
Body Finish Gloss

-Hardware-
Tuning Keys GOTOH GB528
Bridge 18-string ATELIER Z BB518 (18 mm)
Finish Chrome
Pickguard Clear Acrylic 3mm
Control Panel Clear Acrylic, 3mm
Control Knobs ATELIER Z ZAK-1500 ZAK-2000
Neck Plate ATELIER Z 4-Point Plate
Electronics Pickups 18P=ATELIER Z JBZ-5/18Px2
Preamp Bartolini XTCT
Controls MfVol, BAL, MfTone/sw (Active ON/OFF), Treble/Bass/Middle Boost (There is a click at the 50% boost position)
Color Natural

item.title: SPECTOR NS-2 Amber [1198] (07/02) — $7,465.19
item.description:
$7,465.19 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A very early Kramer model, an NS-2 from 1986, has just arrived.

1986 was the very year Spector was acquired by Kramer. The serial number, starting with g119 Brooklyn,h suggests this instrument was built shortly after the acquisition by Kramer.
The solid maple wings feature a subtle yet alluring grain, and the artistic through-neck construction creates a truly exquisite look. Itfs in such excellent condition that itfs hard to believe it was made 40 years ago. The fact that the third-string tuner is mounted at a slight angle gives you a sense of the care that went into this handcrafted instrument.Itfs equipped with the familiar EMG pickups. The caramel-colored backs of the pickups are a hallmark of 1980s EMGs, and the preamp is, of course, made by Haz Lab.

As is typical of vintage instruments, there are signs of wear throughout, but there are no issues affecting playability, and it functions perfectly.
Please donft miss out on this opportunity.

Comes with the original hard case

item.title: FENDER 1971 Jazz Bass Blonde Blonde [335979] (07/02) — $10,751.67
item.description:
$10,751.67 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Pro Bassist's Clearance Sale! A Jazz Bass with a blonde finish has just arrived.

Based on the pickup dates (9521 and 8521), pot dates (304-7116 ~ 3), and the serial number, wefve determined this instrument was manufactured in 1971. While the capacitor has been replaced with a Suntan model and the set screw on the 4-string saddle has been swapped out, no other major parts or the finish have been altered, making this a highly appealing instrument due to its high level of originality.
Above all, the materials are worth noting. As you may have guessed from the gBlondeh finish, this is a rare model constructed from ash and rosewood. The thick, stunning grain visible through the strap buckle marks attests to its authenticity.
The previous owner had the frets and nut replaced, so the playability is impeccable. Since this instrument was actively used on stage until just the other day, its reliability is, of course, outstanding.

This is a truly magnificent instrument that youfre unlikely to ever come across again. It comes with its original black Tolex case.

item.title: Washburn T25NMK Taurus 5-String Bass - Natural Matte — $300
item.description:
$300 · Fair · Jamal's Bass & Gear Outlet

This is a used Washburn Taurus T25 5 String Neck Through Bass in Natural Matte finish. The bass has heavy cosmetic wear as well as the cover to the back control cavity being gone. The previous owner signed the bass in red too? Not sure why lol. All imperfections are shown in the above pictures. Everything else functions as it should and the bass sounds and plays great. I will ship it with a square of black laminate that I was going to cut into a control cavity cover.

The Washburn Taurus T25 is a 5-string electric bass featuring a solid mahogany body, a 24-fret neck-through design, and passive Washburn J-style pickups. It is widely praised for delivering premium neck-through construction, striking tonewoods, and great sustain.


Body & Construction

  • Body Shape: Double cutaway
  • Body Wood: Solid mahogany
  • Finish: Satin / Natural Matte
  • Construction: Neck-through-body 

Neck & Fretboard

  • Neck Wood: Multi-laminate Maple/Mahogany
  • Scale Length: 34 inches
  • Fingerboard Material: Rosewood
  • Number of Frets: 24 medium frets
  • Nut Material: Graphtech Nubone
  • Nut Width: 1.625 inches (42 mm)
  • String Spacing: Approximately 17.5mm

Electronics

  • Pickups: Dual Washburn J-style (Jazz) single-coil pickups
  • Electronics Type: Passive
  • Controls: 2 x Volume, 2 x Tone

Hardware

  • Tuners: Grover or Chrome Die-cast bass tuners
  • Bridge: Hardtail / 5-saddle adjustable
  • Hardware Finish: Chrome

item.title: 1966 Hagstrom IIB Short Scale Bass Sunburst (Made in Sweden) — $795
item.description:
$795 · Very Good · I Want That Guitar

1966 Hagstrom HII-B Short Scale Bass Sunburst (Made in Sweden)

Up for sale, here's a vintage 1966 Hagstrom HII-B short scale bass in Sunburst, made in Sweden, not a reissue. It's in Very Good condition for its age and remains all original with the exception of one replacement knob. It weighs 6.6 lbs and has a comfortable 30" short scale, and it carries its original single-coil pickups along with Hagstrom's signature slide switches, giving access to a few different tonal combinations.

The bass plays fine. The truss rod works as it should, the neck is straight, and the original frets still have plenty of life left in them. The hardware is all original too, including the tuners, the nut, and the wood bridge, though one of the bridge's height-adjustment screws is missing. It's a simple, inexpensive part to source and replace.

Cosmetically the bass is Very Good for its age, showing consistent, honest playwear throughout the body. In a few spots you can see Hagstrom's hot pink polyester primer peeking through, a basecoat the factory used as a sealer beneath the color finish, and it's most visible in body cavities or through small chips in the original lacquer. That pink undercoat showing through is a good sign of a genuine, untouched original finish.

It comes with what we believe to be its original black Tolex hard case with a blue interior.

It ships professionally packed and fully insured to the Continental US and select international destinations.

item.title: Dingwall Super J Whalepool Burst 5-String Bass [Used] — $6,044
item.description:
$6,044 · Excellent · Bella Romeo Music

By adopting a fan fret system of 32" on the G string and 35" on the Low B string, the thinner the string, the more punchy and sweet the tone, and the thicker the string, the faster the rise, giving a clear and accurate pitch feel.

In addition, the unique head structure not only provides optimal tension to the strings at all times, but also reduces the weight of the head so there is no need to worry about the head falling off. The truss rod has a strong structure that can withstand a load of over 400kg. In addition, the carefully applied oil finish gives it a smooth and natural feel, while at the same time creating a neck that is more stable than polyurethane or lacquer finishes, even in the face of rapidly changing environments. I am.

This machine is the Super J, which was developed by Dingwall to improve the string tension balance that conventional jazz basses had. It features an alder body, a bolt-on 5pc maple neck/maple fingerboard, and a passive FD3 pickup and glockenklang 3-band EQ preamp. This bass weighs 4.3kg.

Thanks to the tension balance of each string created by the fan fret, each string can be played with the same force, and the tone balance is also unified, making it an instrument that allows you to concentrate on playing without being tied to a particular position. With the mid-low range derived from alder and the clear tone of maple, his sound has a wide range yet is typical of bass, and has the potential to be used in any genre.

This bass is used and is in Excellent+ condition.  There are a few signs of usage, such as light scratches and small nicks, and it is in beautiful condition with no significant wear. There is almost no wear on the frets. It has plenty of remaining truss rod left and right and is in good playing condition. This bass comes with a Reunion branded gig bag, which is well padded for the road.

This item is listed on Reverb.com as well as being sold Bellaromeo online store and our physical showroom in Wan Chai, Hong Kong. In rare occasions, the item may be sold out first via other channels, in which case we regrettably will need to prioritise the order fulfilment by the time stamp of the order being placed on a first-come-first-sold basis.  You may find  additional images and item specifications on Bellaromeo online store.

item.title: Dingwall Super J Olympic White 5-string Bass [Used] — $6,484
item.description:
$6,484 · Excellent · Bella Romeo Music

DINGAWALL's JB style model Super J, equipped with the now rare single coil size Super Fatty pickups.

By adopting a fan fret system of 32" for the G string and 35" for the Low B string, the thinner the string, the more punchy and sweet the tone, and the thicker the string, the faster the rise, giving a clear and accurate sense of pitch. Succeeded in reproducing the tone accompanied by This is a highly complete instrument that can express nuances beautifully.

In addition, the unique head structure not only provides optimal tension to the strings at all times, but also reduces the weight of the head so there is no need to worry about the head falling off. The truss rod has a strong structure that can withstand a load of over 400kg. In addition, the carefully applied oil finish gives it a smooth and natural feel, while at the same time creating a neck that is more stable than polyurethane or lacquer finishes, even in the face of rapidly changing environments.

This Bass is the Super J, which was developed by Dingwall to improve the string tension balance that conventional jazz basses had. It has an alder body and a bolt-on 5pc maple neck/Morado fingerboard. The electrical system is equipped with a hum-cancelling Super Fatty pickup and glockenspiel crank preamp. The Super Fatty has a modern wide range, but as its name suggests, it has a strong mid-low range, giving it a wide and natural tone.

Because it uses an Alder body specification, with a Morado (Pau Perro) fingerboard on a maple neck.  This instrument tends to have a rich middle and low sound, but it also has the tension balance of each string that is unique to the Fanned-Fret System. , a good pitch feel, and you'll experience a good balance that you can't get with a normal bass. Speaking of Dingwall, the Low-B string has a 35-inch scale, which is a short scale among models of the same brand, but the good pronunciation, clear pitch, and smooth rise are unique to Dingwall. I think he is. It can be said that the balance is the result of a combination of various factors, such as the strength of the neck and the high precision of its assembly. In addition, while most Dingwall instruments seem to be lightweight, this one weighs approximately 4.1kg, which is one of the lightest for a 5-string bass. The Baltic Amber color pickguard, which is rarely seen these days, also contributes to the cool look.

This bass is used and is in Excellent+ Condition. Although there is some discoloration on the paint and dullness on the pegs, there is only one nick on the end of the body's back, and it is in good condition with no other noticeable scratches. There is almost no wear on the frets, and the truss rod works smoothly and with plenty of room. There are no problems with the condition of the neck. We will keep it in a condition that will allow you to use it for many years to come.  This bass comes with its original gig bag, toolkit/truss rod tool, and inspection papers.


This item is listed on Reverb.com as well as being sold Bellaromeo online store and our physical showroom in Wan Chai, Hong Kong. item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX21 VJ5 TBT/R 4.16kg #SMX J 000351-25【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,442
item.description:
$1,442 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

This is the MX21 VJ5, a 5-string model adopting the classic JB style.

The body is made of lightweight solid Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).

This is a standard combination in the MX series.

The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.

The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.

The pickups are passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky stacked J-Style pickups.

While retaining the sharp, crisp attack characteristic of JB-type basses, the hum-cancellation suppresses noise, resulting in a clear sound.

The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.

Bass and treble frequencies can be significantly boosted with simple controls.

The preamp can also be bypassed via a push/pull knob.

The preamp's circuit board is almost identical to that of top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.

Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.

These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.

Even at this price point, Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising dedication to instrument making is evident.

While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, the sound is truly that of a refined JB. This bass delivers a modern tone that stands out in an ensemble, with rich harmonics, crisp highs perfect for slap bass, and a tight, well-tuned Low-B note that blends seamlessly.

Based on the traditional JB bass, this instrument incorporates modern elements, reflecting Roger Sadowsky's philosophy.

SPECIFICATION

Scale length: 34" (864 mm)
Construction: Bolt-On
Body Wood: Okoume
Neck Wood: Roasted Maple
Fingerboard: Morado (Pau Ferro)
Fingerboard Radius: 12"
Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Nut Width: 1.875" (47.6 mm)
Frets: 21 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051" (1.3 mm))
Neck Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky stacked J-Style
Bridge Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky stacked J-Style in 60s position
Electronics: Sadowsky Active 2-Way
Controls: Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass (Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp (Bypass)
Tuners: Sadowsky Light machine heads with open gear
Security Locks: Sadowsky S-style
Bridge System: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
Hardware Color: Chrome
Strings: 045 - 135 Stainless Steel
Included Accessories: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector and Hex Key Tools
Gig Bag: Sadowsky Portabag (SAC BAG BASS PORTA)

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Product Photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made in Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: 2019 Fender American Elite Jazz Bass - Killer Active Jazz! — $1,600
item.description:
$1,600 · Very Good · L.A. Vintage Gear

2019 Fender American Elite Jazz Bass finished in the eye-catching Satin Ice Blue Metallic with a beautiful maple fingerboard.


Designed for the modern bassist, the American Elite series blends classic Jazz Bass styling with contemporary performance features, creating an instrument that is equally at home on stage, in the studio, or on the road. From its sleek contours to its effortless playability, every aspect of the Elite is built with versatility and precision in mind.


The compound-radius maple fingerboard and comfortable modern "C" to "D" neck profile provide a fast, natural playing experience across the entire neck, while the sculpted neck heel offers unrestricted access to the upper frets. Fender's fourth-generation Noiseless Jazz Bass pickups deliver the unmistakable growl, punch, and articulation of a classic J-Bass without the hum, and the onboard 18-volt active preamp with active/passive switching provides an incredibly wide range of usable tones.


The bass is in very good condition with some notable dings and blemishes (shown in photos). Includes the original Fender Elite molded hardshell case and accompanying case candy.


Specs:

  • Body Material: Alder

  • Neck: Maple, Compound Back Shape, Modern “C” to “D”

  • Neck Finish: Satin Urethane with Gloss Urethane Headstock Face

  • Fingerboard: Maple, 9.5” to 14” Compound Radius

  • Nut (Material/Width): Bone, 1.5”

  • Pickups: New 4th Generation Noiseless (Bridge), New 4th Generation Noiseless (Middle)

  • Pickup Switching: 2-Way Mini Toggle Switch for Active/Passive Mode. In Passive Mode, Only The Volume, Pan and Passive Tone Controls Function. In Active Mode, All Controls Function.

  • Special Electronics: 18V Preamp with Active 3-Band EQ

  • Controls: Master Volume, Pan Pot (Pickup Selector), Treble Boost/Cut, Midrange Boost/Cut, Bass Boost/Cut, Active/Passive Mini Toggle

  • Weight - 9.5 lbs

item.title: Xotic XJ-1T 6st Ash/M -Natural- #3862[4.62kg ][Made in Japan][IKE011] — $2,695.94
item.description:
$2,695.94 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----
*Caution!!
To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.
Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.
-----


Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.


----------------------------------

A must-have for Xotic fans has just arrived!

A rare 6-string model of the “XJ-1T,” which is already scarce on the market. Among these, the natural-colored instrument we’ve just received features a beautiful grain that fully showcases the powerful, distinctive character unique to ash wood.


Xotic seamlessly blends a deep respect for vintage basses with the functionality and playability demanded by today’s music scene. The brand is also known for being highly regarded by top professional musicians, including Chuck Rainey and Travis Carlton.


This instrument is truly a player’s model, distilling the very essence of Xotic’s appeal.


It leverages the vast dynamic range unique to a 6-string bass to deliver overwhelming expressiveness and practical sound-shaping capabilities.


First and foremost, its superb sound balance deserves special mention!


In passive mode, in addition to the clear, open tone produced by the ash body and maple neck, it faithfully reproduces every nuance of your picking and fingering. You’ll experience a responsiveness that seems to translate the player’s expressiveness directly into sound.


And when you switch to active mode, its character changes completely!


The 3-band EQ allows for a wide range of sound shaping, giving you complete control over everything from a fat, punchy modern sound to a sparkling tone that shines during slap playing, and a punchy sound that cuts through the mix.


Furthermore, it features a mini-switch that lets you toggle between mid and treble frequencies, boasting overwhelming versatility that makes this single instrument suitable for live performances, recording, and any genre.


“I want a vintage-style jazz bass sound too.”

“I also need a modern active sound”

“I want to play solos that make the most of the 6-string’s range”


This versatile instrument meets all these demanding needs.


Its excellent playability is also a standout feature.


The medium-weight design, crafted from carefully selected ash wood, delivers a rich low end and stable low-midrange. Furthermore, the use of Hipshot Ultra Light tuners ensures excellent weight balance, even for a 6-string bass.


You’ll hardly notice the headstock drooping even when playing standing up, ensuring a comfortable playing experience during long live sets and rehearsals.


And one of Xotic’s greatest selling points is the inclusion of the HRI (Hum Reduction Inductor).


It significantly reduces hum while maintaining the open, natural sound characteristic of single-coil pickups. It’s exquisitely tuned to deliver a noise-free sound without sacrificing the inherent response and airiness of single-coil pickups.


Furthermore, since the HRI is a passive circuit, it requires no batteries and remains effective even in passive mode.


This will be a major advantage in both clean recording environments and on live stages.


From chord work and solo playing to gospel, fusion, pop, rock, and R&B.


The XJ-1T combines the expressiveness demanded of a 6-string bass with practical performance capable of withstanding professional use.


In recent years, 6-string models have been extremely rare on the market, and opportunities to find one in good condition are limited.


This particular instrument is especially appealing, featuring a beautiful ash grain and excellent tonal balance.


We highly recommend this model to both those who primarily use passive basses and those who have previously been hesitant to try active basses.


If you’re looking for a 6-string bass that expands your musical possibilities, don’t miss this opportunity.

~Specifications~

BODY: Ash

PICKUP LAYOUT: J-J

STRAP BUTTON: Gotoh

BRIDGE: Xotic A style Brass(19mm)

NECK: 22F Maple

NECK FINISH: Oil

NUT: Pure Bone(51mm)

GRIP: C-shape

FRET: #6150type Jescar Silver Nickel

POSITION MARK: luminoba

TUNER: Hip Shot LIC Ultra Light Clover

PICK UPS: Xotic JB 6st

HRI(Hum Reduction Inductor)：Included

CONTROLS: Master Vol(Pull=Passive), PU Blancer, Master Tone, 3-Band EQ Xotic Bass Preamp

(Mid/Bright SW, Output Gain Control)

PICKUP COVER: Rosewood


Weight: Approx. 4.62kg

Included Accessories: Gig bag

Our custom-order item has finally arrived after a one-year wait!!


[Custom Order Details]

We placed the order for this custom model—one of our store’s custom-order models—around May 2025.

The color chosen for this model, as the name suggests, is a blue hue inspired by the body color of a Mercedes-Benz automobile.

Finished with a lacquer coating, this dark, distinctive blue features a deep, subdued tone with just the right amount of aged finishing, resulting in an elegant color brimming with vintage charm.

Combined with Xotic’s “Medium Aged” treatment, the guitar boasts a truly classic and captivating look.

The aged finish extends down to the finest details—including dents on the body and headstock, fine cracks crisscrossing the surface, and areas where the primer is visible on the elbow—creating a realistic texture that suggests the guitar has actually been in use for nearly 60 years.

Furthermore, the matching headstock design lends a sense of overall unity to the instrument. We selected a black 3-piece pickguard to achieve a traditional look unique to custom-order models.

By achieving an “aged finish that’s just right—not overdone,” we’ve created a masterpiece that maintains elegance while fully exuding character, and the person in charge is absolutely delighted with the result!!

We highly recommend this guitar—we’d love for you to play music on it!!

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.


----------------------------------


Xotic instruments are crafted to the highest standards, blending vintage aesthetics with modern playability.

Thanks to their solid craftsmanship and sound—recognized by studio musicians such as Chuck Rainey and Travis Carlton—they continue to gain fans among both professionals and amateurs alike.


This particular instrument, a custom model made exclusively for our store, features an ash body that excels in delivering a wide dynamic range.

With the scarcity of ash wood becoming a growing concern in recent years, even when the wood is available, it’s difficult to find pieces that aren’t extremely heavy or that feature beautiful grain patterns.

Despite these challenges, the ash used in this instrument boasts a grain pattern so distinct it could serve as a textbook example, giving it a truly beautiful appearance.


By pairing a white blonde finish—which maximizes the beauty of the ash—with a black pickguard, we’ve achieved a cohesive yet classic look!


The XJ-1T combines such a wide range of tonal possibilities and playability that people say, “With this one guitar, you can handle anything.” Please take this opportunity to try it out.


We look forward to your inquiries.


Below is the description of the XJ-1T model.


The XJ-1T enables a wide range of sound shaping, from vintage to modern.

Its distinctive control layout includes 2 Volume, 1 Tone, and a 3-band EQ. Pulling the front volume pot switches the guitar to passive mode, while pulling the rear volume pot connects the pickups in series to produce a Precision-like sound.

The two mini-switches control the mid and treble frequency ranges, respectively, enabling versatile tone shaping that lets this single guitar handle multiple roles.


The guitar is equipped with “Raw Vintage” pickups, each meticulously hand-wired by artisans in a workshop in Los Angeles, California.

Even in passive mode, the balanced sound faithfully conveys the nuances of your touch, and as you boost the EQ, it transforms into a modern sound with strong presence.


The body is crafted from ash selected for its medium weight, delivering a sound rich in low to low-mid frequencies. Combined with lightweight tuning machines, it offers excellent body balance when held.


This is a model we highly recommend to players who primarily use passive models, as well as those who have been hesitant to try active pickups in the past.


We look forward to your inquiries.


~Specifications~

BODY: Ash

PICKUP LAYOUT: J-J

STRAP BUTTON: Gotoh

BRIDGE: Xotic A-style Brass (19mm)

NECK: 22F Maple/Maple

NECK FINISH: Oil

NUT: Pure Bone (47mm)

GRIP: C-shape

FRETS: #6150-type Jescar Silver Nickel

POSITION MARK: Luminoba

TUNER: Hip Shot LIC Ultra Light Clover

PICKUPS: Raw Vintage

CONTROLS: 2 Volume, 1 Tone, 3-Band EQ Xotic Bass Preamp

(Mid/Bright Switch, Output Gain Control)

PICKUP COVER: Rosewood

FINISH: Poly


Weight: Approx. 4.45 kg

Included Accessories: Gig bag
